text:
There's a forest where birds sing songs that talk like humans.

Once upon a time, a wise owl called Orion. Orion loved to listen to the gossip birds.

One evening, the chatterboxes complained about a bad winter.

"It doesn't rain! Even the songs stopped!" they moaned.

Orion thought that perhaps a drop did rain from his own feathers.

Maybe he felt a drop.

"I think it's just a fancy feather."

Orion didn't believe that.

"The stars? Just dreams?"

No stars.

"You really think..." Orion mumbled.

Just a little drop rain...

"The rain..." he repeated softly.

Then a songbird landed on his head.

"Then hear me!"

The songbird hummed softly.

"Who sings like you?" Orion said.

Just a bird.

"Then sing!"

Just a few notes.

"Not like anything!"

Orion pondered that.

A shadow swooped down.

A badger.

"I hear your complains," the badger said.

"Oh,
